title: Scan an Image for Vulnerabilities
story: You've built an image `legacy-app:v1`. Use Docker Scout or Trivy to scan it for critical CVEs, then rebuild using an updated base image to eliminate the critical vulnerabilities.
objectives: Scan `legacy-app:v1` (pre-built with old ubuntu base) and identify critical vulnerabilities
Rebuild with updated base image `ubuntu:22.04` as `legacy-app:v2`
Scan `legacy-app:v2` and confirm zero critical CVEs
hints: `docker scout cves legacy-app:v1` or `trivy image legacy-app:v1`
Change `FROM ubuntu:18.04` to `FROM ubuntu:22.04` in the Dockerfile

initialState.summary: - `legacy-app:v1` pre-built using `ubuntu:18.04` (has known CVEs) - `/workspace/Dockerfile` available for editing

workspaceFiles.content:
FROM ubuntu:18.04
RUN apt-get update
RUN apt-get install -y curl
RUN apt-get install -y wget
CMD ["bash", "-lc", "sleep 3600"]
